overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to social media analysis, disinformation, and computational social science. LinkedIn accounts for the largest follower base, while engagement varies across platforms depending on content type and audience.

contentThemes.description: Sharing research findings, publications, and grant awards related to social media analysis and disinformation.
Posting open positions for postdoctoral researchers and PhD students in the field.
Announcing upcoming presentations and involvement in relevant conferences.
Announcements regarding software releases and updates.

recommendations: Increase cross-promotion of content across platforms to reach a wider audience.
Experiment with different content formats (e.g., videos, infographics) to boost engagement.
Actively engage with comments and questions to foster a stronger sense of community.
Use platform-specific hashtags to improve content discoverability.
Schedule posts strategically to align with peak audience activity times on each platform.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky demonstrates good engagement through reposts, particularly with posts relating to research opportunities and publications. Focus on continuing to share research outputs and actively engaging with replies to foster community involvement.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon has the smallest follower count and engagement rate, suggesting the need to tailor content more specifically to this platform's audience. Focus on sharing more informal insights and engaging in relevant community discussions.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest follower base and the highest overall engagement, driven by professional announcements and job postings. Continue leveraging this platform for career-related updates and sharing insights relevant to industry professionals.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
